title: Centre Lifts Petrol and Diesel Sale Restrictions from July 1, 2026
summary: The Central Government has decided to lift restrictions on the sale of petrol and diesel effective July 1, 2026, as supply concerns ease. These restrictions were described as 'war-time curbs', suggesting they were imposed in the context of the India-Pakistan conflict in May 2026. The move signals normalization of fuel supply chains and domestic energy markets.
exam_angle: Relates to Essential Commodities Act powers, government control over petroleum product distribution, and energy security policy. Also connects to the broader India-Pakistan conflict context and its economic aftereffects.
key_facts: Restrictions lifted effective: July 1, 2026
Restrictions were termed 'war-time curbs' on petrol/diesel sale
Decision taken as domestic supply concerns eased
Announced by: Central Government
source: The Times of India / The Hindu

title: CBSE Issues Three-Language Policy Guidelines for Classes 7–9; Allows Two Foreign Language Options
summary: CBSE has relaxed its three-language policy for Classes 7, 8, and 9, now permitting students to choose two foreign languages as part of their language study. The guidelines also carry significant updates for Class 10 students. Karnataka's state school body opposed the CBSE notification, arguing that Kannada cannot be treated as a third language in the state.
exam_angle: Directly linked to the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020's three-language formula and its implementation challenges. The opposition from Karnataka highlights centre-state tensions over education policy, a key GS Paper 2 topic.
key_facts: Issued by: Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE)
Applicable to: Classes 7, 8, and 9
Change: Two foreign language options now permitted under three-language policy
Opposition: Karnataka state school body objects to treating Kannada as third language

title: PM Modi Addresses Seychelles National Assembly, Attends 50th Independence Anniversary Celebrations
summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi visited Seychelles and addressed its National Assembly, reiterating India's role as Seychelles' 'trusted partner'. He also attended the 50th Anniversary celebrations of Seychelles' independence and met members of the Indian diaspora. The visit underscores India's SAGAR (Security and Growth for All in the Region) doctrine and strategic engagement in the Indian Ocean Region.
exam_angle: Key for India's Indian Ocean diplomacy and SAGAR policy. Seychelles is strategically important for India's maritime security interests; PM's address to a foreign parliament is a notable diplomatic gesture.
key_facts: PM Modi addressed: Seychelles National Assembly
Occasion: 50th Anniversary of Seychelles' Independence
India described as Seychelles' 'trusted partner'
Seychelles is a key partner under India's SAGAR doctrine in the Indian Ocean Region
source: The New Indian Express / The Hindu

title: India Distances Itself from Reports of Track-2 Dialogue with Pakistan; Indus Waters Treaty Cited as New Flashpoint
summary: The Indian government clarified it has no official involvement in any Track-2 diplomatic dialogue with Pakistan, distancing itself from media reports of unofficial back-channel talks. Simultaneously, the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) emerged as a fresh flashpoint following speculation triggered by these Track-2 discussions. India had put the IWT in abeyance following the Pahalgam attack in April 2026.
exam_angle: Critical for India-Pakistan relations, particularly the status of the Indus Waters Treaty (1960, World Bank-mediated). Track-2 diplomacy as a concept and IWT's legal/strategic dimensions are frequently examined.
key_facts: India clarified: No official involvement in Track-2 talks with Pakistan
Indus Waters Treaty (1960) identified as new India-Pakistan flashpoint
IWT was put in abeyance by India following the April 2026 Pahalgam attack
Track-2 dialogue: unofficial, non-governmental dialogue between two countries

title: India Sends Bihar Governor and MoS External Affairs to Ayatollah Khamenei's Funeral in Iran
summary: India decided to send Bihar Governor Lt. Gen. Syed Ata Hasnain and Minister of State for External Affairs Pabitra Margherita as its official representatives to the funeral of Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Khamenei. The delegation's composition reflects the diplomatic significance India attaches to its ties with Iran. Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei passed away recently.
exam_angle: India-Iran bilateral relations, Iran's strategic importance to India (Chabahar Port, energy imports), and the significance of diplomatic representation at state funerals are relevant topics.
key_facts: India's delegation: Bihar Governor Lt. Gen. Syed Ata Hasnain and MoS External Affairs Pabitra Margherita
Occasion: Funeral of Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Khamenei
Iran is a key strategic partner for India (Chabahar Port, energy)

title: US and Iran Agree to Halt Strikes; Trump Announces Meeting in Qatar Amid West Asia Tensions
summary: The United States and Iran agreed to halt mutual strikes and hold talks in Doha, Qatar, following a period of military skirmishes. President Trump announced the meeting even as Iran stated no technical-level meeting was immediately expected. The development is a significant de-escalation signal in the ongoing West Asia conflict, which has implications for global oil supply and India's energy security.
exam_angle: West Asia geopolitics directly affects India's energy security, diaspora safety, and remittances. The US-Iran nuclear standoff and Gulf stability are standard IR topics for UPSC.
key_facts: US and Iran agreed to halt strikes and hold talks in Doha, Qatar
Meeting announced by President Trump despite Iran's reservations
Context: Military skirmishes between US and Iran in West Asia
Implications for India: energy security, diaspora in Gulf region

title: China Responds to India's Concerns Over Teesta River Cooperation with Bangladesh
summary: China formally responded to India's expressed concerns regarding its cooperation with Bangladesh on the Teesta River project. India has been wary of China's growing infrastructure presence in Bangladesh, particularly on the Teesta river management project which India considers within its strategic sphere. The Teesta flows through West Bengal before entering Bangladesh, making it a sensitive transboundary water issue.
exam_angle: Teesta river dispute involves India-Bangladesh relations, transboundary water sharing, and China's Belt and Road Initiative footprint in South Asia — all key IR and geography topics.
key_facts: Issue: China's involvement in Teesta River cooperation project with Bangladesh
India has raised concerns over China's growing strategic presence in Bangladesh
Teesta River: originates in Sikkim, flows through West Bengal into Bangladesh
Connects to: BRI presence in India's neighbourhood and India's 'Neighbourhood First' policy

title: Kashmir SIA Files 737-Page Chargesheet Naming Yasin Malik as Main Accused in 1990 Sarla Bhat Rape-Murder Case
summary: The State Investigation Agency (SIA) of Jammu & Kashmir filed a 737-page chargesheet naming JKLF chief Yasin Malik as the main accused in the 1990 rape and murder of Kashmiri Pandit nurse Sarla Bhat. Four other JKLF operatives were also named. The case, 36 years old, is now before a court, marking a significant step in accountability for crimes against Kashmiri Pandits during the exodus of 1990.
exam_angle: Connects to the Kashmiri Pandit exodus of 1990, NIA/SIA jurisdiction in terrorism cases, and the broader issue of justice for victims of terrorism. Yasin Malik is already serving a life sentence in the Pulwama conspiracy case.
key_facts: Filed by: State Investigation Agency (SIA), Jammu & Kashmir
Chargesheet length: 737 pages
Main accused: Yasin Malik, chief of Jammu Kashmir Liberation Front (JKLF)
Victim: Sarla Bhat, a Kashmiri Pandit nurse; crime occurred in 1990 during the Kashmiri Pandit exodus
source: The Indian Express / The New Indian Express

title: Orissa High Court Rules Written Grounds of Arrest are Mandatory
summary: The Orissa High Court ruled that providing written grounds of arrest to an accused is mandatory and not merely a formality. This ruling aligns with the Supreme Court's landmark judgment in Prabir Purkayastha v. State (2024) and the constitutional mandate under Article 22(1), which requires that an arrested person be informed of the grounds of arrest. The ruling reinforces fundamental rights of the accused.
exam_angle: Directly tests Article 22 of the Constitution (protection against arbitrary arrest and detention), the right to be informed of grounds of arrest, and recent Supreme Court jurisprudence on personal liberty.
key_facts: Court: Orissa High Court
Ruling: Written grounds of arrest are mandatory
Constitutional basis: Article 22(1) — right to be informed of grounds of arrest
Aligns with Supreme Court ruling in Prabir Purkayastha v. State (2024)

title: Cabinet Approves Additional Rs. 30,000 Crore Investment Commitment in National Investment and Infrastructure Fund (NIIF)
summary: The Union Cabinet approved an additional investment commitment of Rs. 30,000 crore in the National Investment and Infrastructure Fund (NIIF) to accelerate infrastructure investment and catalyse institutional capital into India. NIIF is India's sovereign wealth fund established in 2015 to fund commercially viable infrastructure projects. This fresh infusion aims to attract long-term domestic and foreign capital into infrastructure.
exam_angle: NIIF is a frequently examined topic — its structure as a quasi-sovereign wealth fund, mandate, and role in infrastructure financing under the National Infrastructure Pipeline (NIP) are key facts.
key_facts: Approved by: Union Cabinet
Amount: Additional Rs. 30,000 crore investment commitment
Fund: National Investment and Infrastructure Fund (NIIF)
NIIF established: 2015; functions as India's sovereign wealth fund for infrastructure

title: India Seeks Relief from EU on Scrap Export Curbs Amid Free Trade Agreement Negotiations
summary: India has approached the European Union seeking relief from EU restrictions on scrap exports, warning that such curbs threaten to undermine gains from the ongoing India-EU Free Trade Agreement (FTA) negotiations. EU restrictions on scrap exports — particularly ferrous and non-ferrous metals — affect India's steel and recycling industries. The issue has become a sticking point in India-EU trade talks.
exam_angle: India-EU FTA negotiations, circular economy regulations (EU's Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism and scrap export rules), and their impact on India's manufacturing sector are relevant for GS 3 and Essay.
key_facts: India requested EU relief from scrap export restrictions
Issue threatens gains from India-EU Free Trade Agreement negotiations
Affected sectors: Indian steel and recycling industries
EU scrap restrictions are linked to its circular economy and sustainability regulations

title: Delhi Cabinet Approves New EV Policy: Only Electric Autos from 2027, E-Two-Wheelers from 2028
summary: The Delhi Cabinet cleared a new Electric Vehicle (EV) policy mandating that only electric auto-rickshaws will be permitted to operate in Delhi from 2027, and e-two-wheelers will be mandatory from 2028. The policy is aimed at reducing air pollution in the national capital and accelerating the transition to clean mobility. Delhi is one of the most polluted cities globally and faces annual air quality crises.
exam_angle: EV policy connects to urban air quality, climate commitments (net-zero goals), the PM e-Bus Sewa scheme, and centre-state roles in pollution control — key for Environment and Governance.
key_facts: Policy approved by: Delhi Cabinet
Only electric auto-rickshaws permitted from: 2027
Only e-two-wheelers permitted from: 2028
Objective: Reduce air pollution in Delhi; accelerate EV transition

title: Flash Floods Wreak Havoc in Assam and Arunachal Pradesh; Over 22,000 Affected, Railway Bridge Collapses
summary: Flash floods caused widespread destruction in Assam and Arunachal Pradesh, affecting over 22,000 people and leading to the collapse of a railway bridge. Union Home Minister Amit Shah contacted Assam CM Himanta Biswa Sarma to assess the flood situation. Assam faces recurring annual floods due to the Brahmaputra river system, and experts have called for a Climate Mobility Plan for the region.
exam_angle: Assam floods are a perennial disaster management issue — relevant to NDMA, Brahmaputra river dynamics, climate change-induced extreme weather events, and Article 275 special grants for Assam.
key_facts: Affected states: Assam and Arunachal Pradesh
People affected: Over 22,000
Infrastructure damage: Railway bridge collapsed
Home Minister Amit Shah contacted Assam CM Himanta Biswa Sarma regarding the situation

title: India's First Offshore Airport to Come Up in Maharashtra
summary: India is set to build its first offshore airport in Maharashtra, a project that would be built on reclaimed land in the sea. Offshore airports are rare globally — notable examples include Kansai Airport in Japan and Hong Kong International Airport. The project is expected to have significant economic implications for Maharashtra and India's aviation sector.
exam_angle: Infrastructure development, land reclamation technology, and Maharashtra's role as India's economic hub are relevant. Offshore airports relate to topics of coastal regulation, environmental clearances, and connectivity.
key_facts: India's first offshore airport location: Maharashtra
Type: Built on reclaimed land in the sea
Global precedents: Kansai Airport (Japan), Hong Kong International Airport
Significance: Major boost to India's aviation infrastructure

title: India's Green Hydrogen from Nuclear Reactor: BARC Initiative for Clean Fuel
summary: India has taken a significant step towards clean fuel production by generating green hydrogen using a nuclear reactor, an initiative by the Bhabha Atomic Research Centre (BARC). This approach uses nuclear energy — which is low-carbon — to power electrolysis for hydrogen production, potentially lowering costs compared to solar/wind-based green hydrogen. It aligns with India's National Green Hydrogen Mission.
exam_angle: National Green Hydrogen Mission (launched 2023, target: 5 MMT by 2030), BARC's role in nuclear research, and the intersection of nuclear energy with decarbonization goals are key sci-tech and environment topics.
key_facts: Initiative by: Bhabha Atomic Research Centre (BARC)
Technology: Green hydrogen produced using nuclear reactor-powered electrolysis
Aligns with: National Green Hydrogen Mission (2023)
India's green hydrogen target: 5 Million Metric Tonnes (MMT) per year by 2030

title: Army Raises Specialised 'Baaz' Battalions for Drone-Based Surveillance and Long-Range Operations
summary: The Indian Army has raised specialised 'Baaz' battalions dedicated to sustained drone-based surveillance and long-range operations. Additionally, NDA Khadakwasla has set up a drone training laboratory to prepare officer cadets for future warfare scenarios. The Army Chief also spoke about future warfare preparedness and China in an exclusive interview.
exam_angle: Drone warfare and unmanned systems are rapidly evolving defence topics. Baaz battalions reflect India's shift to technology-intensive military doctrine, relevant to India's Defence Acquisition Procedure and Atmanirbhar Bharat in defence.
key_facts: Indian Army raised: Specialised 'Baaz' battalions for drone surveillance and long-range ops
NDA Khadakwasla established: Drone training laboratory for officer cadets
Focus: Future warfare preparedness using unmanned aerial systems (UAS)
Context: Post-Operation Sindoor lessons on drone warfare

title: TRL Compass Platform Launched by Principal Scientific Adviser's Office; MoU Signed with DSCI
summary: The Office of the Principal Scientific Adviser (PSA) to the Government of India launched the TRL (Technology Readiness Level) Compass Platform, a tool designed to assess and track the maturity of Indian technologies. Simultaneously, the PSA office signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) with the Data Security Council of India (DSCI) to strengthen data governance and technology development frameworks.
exam_angle: The TRL framework is used globally to assess technology maturity from concept to deployment. The PSA office's role in India's science and technology ecosystem and DSCI's mandate in data security are relevant for Sci-Tech.
key_facts: Launched by: Office of the Principal Scientific Adviser (PSA) to Government of India
Platform: TRL (Technology Readiness Level) Compass — tracks technology maturity
MoU signed with: Data Security Council of India (DSCI)
Purpose: Strengthen India's technology development and data governance frameworks

title: India Women's Cricket Team Qualifies for LA 2028 Olympics Despite T20 World Cup Early Exit
summary: The Indian women's cricket team has secured an automatic qualification berth for the 2028 Los Angeles Olympics despite being knocked out of the Women's T20 World Cup in the group stage by Australia. India qualified as one of the top-ranked teams automatically. Australia, Great Britain, India, and South Africa were the first four teams to secure Olympic quotas for women's cricket at LA 2028. Cricket is making its Olympic debut at LA 2028.
exam_angle: Cricket's inclusion in the LA 2028 Olympics (T20 format) is a landmark moment. Olympic qualification criteria and India's ICC rankings are relevant for persons-in-news and sports governance questions.
key_facts: India women's cricket team: Qualified automatically for LA 2028 Olympics
First four qualifiers: Australia, Great Britain, India, South Africa
Cricket format at LA 2028: T20
Cricket is making its Olympic debut at the 2028 Los Angeles Games

title: Harshavardhan GB Becomes India's 97th Chess Grandmaster
summary: Harshavardhan GB has become India's 97th Chess Grandmaster, adding to India's remarkable run of producing top-level chess talent. India has emerged as a global chess powerhouse, with reigning world champion D. Gukesh and multiple top-10 ranked players. Harshavardhan's achievement reflects the depth of India's chess ecosystem.
exam_angle: India's chess achievements are frequently featured in persons-in-news. The country's total grandmaster count and major recent achievements (D. Gukesh as world champion) are key facts to memorise.
key_facts: Harshavardhan GB: India's 97th Chess Grandmaster
India is among the top chess-producing nations globally
Recent landmark: D. Gukesh became the youngest World Chess Champion in 2024
Chess governing body: FIDE (Fédération Internationale des Échecs)

title: South Korea Eyes 'Second Wave' of Investment in India, Focusing on Shipbuilding and Defence
summary: South Korea is planning a 'second wave' of investment in India, with a strategic focus on shipbuilding and defence sectors. This comes amid growing India-South Korea defence and industrial cooperation. India's push for Atmanirbhar Bharat in defence and the need for naval shipbuilding capacity make South Korean expertise particularly relevant.
exam_angle: India-South Korea relations, the CEPA (Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement) between the two countries, and South Korea's role in India's defence-industrial complex are relevant IR and economy topics.
key_facts: South Korea plans 'second wave' of investment in India
Focus sectors: Shipbuilding and defence
India-South Korea have a CEPA (Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement)
Connects to India's Atmanirbhar Bharat in defence and naval modernisation

title: Union Health Minister Nadda Chairs 16th Conference of Central Council of Health and Family Welfare
summary: Union Health Minister J.P. Nadda chaired the 16th Conference of the Central Council of Health and Family Welfare (CCHFW), a key body that advises the central and state governments on health policy. The conference serves as a platform for centre-state coordination on public health matters, review of ongoing health schemes, and setting health sector priorities.
exam_angle: The Central Council of Health and Family Welfare (CCHFW) is a constitutional/statutory body under the Ministry of Health. Centre-state coordination in health (a concurrent subject) and national health programmes are core GS Paper 2 topics.
key_facts: Chaired by: Union Health Minister J.P. Nadda
Conference: 16th Conference of the Central Council of Health and Family Welfare (CCHFW)
CCHFW role: Advisory body for health policy to central and state governments
Health is a Concurrent List subject (Schedule VII of the Constitution)
